title: Medical Radiology and Imaging Professionals (General) Regulations, 2010
title_en: Medical Radiology and Imaging Professionals (General) Regulations, 2010

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These Regulations may be cited as the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professionals (General) Regulations, 2010 and shall come into operation on the publication date.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these Regulations unless the context requires otherwise: " Act " means the Radiology and Imaging Act; " Council " means the Radiology and Imaging Council established under section 4 of the Acl " Committee " means a committee established under section 7 of the Act ; " Certificate of enlisting " means the certificate issued under section 28 of the Act ; " Certificate of enrollment " means the certificate issued under section 23 ; " Certificate of registration " means a certificate issued under section 14 of the Act ; " Continuing Professional Development " means the process by which Radiology and Imaging Professionals continuously enhance their knowledge, skills and personal qualities through their professional careers and encompasses a range of activities including continuing education and supplementary training: and " Internship " means the practical training undertaken by a graduate Radiographer and Radiotherapist in terms of a contract under the direct supervision of a Registered Radiology and Imaging Professional al an institution registered as a provider for the qualification in Radiology, and Imaging sciences. Part II – Provisions relating to registration enrolment and enlisting or licensing

provisions.title: Registration
provisions.content: (1) A person shall, pursuant to the provisions of Part III of the Act , be entitled to be registered as a Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional if he: (a) has attained a postgraduate qualification in Radiology and Imaging sciences and has attained a qualification of becoming a Radiologist or Radiation Oncologist from the Institution recognized by the Council ; (b) has attained a postgraduate qualification in Medical Physics from an institution recognized by the Council and is qualified to earn out duties of a Medical Physicist; (c) has attained a postrgaduate qualifications in Radiopharmacy from an institution recognized by the Council and earn out duties of a Radio-pharmacist; (d) has attained a degree in bachelor of science, advanced diploma or diploma in Medical Radiology and Imaging sciences from a training institution recognized by the council: (e) in the case of a degree holder, has undergone an uninterrupted one year internship in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences facility recognized by the council; and (f) in the case of a person other than a degree holder, the additional requirements shall include evidence to satisfy the council that he has attained a provisional registration of not less than one year. (2) In addition to the provisions of Sections 17(1) , 18 and 19 of the Act, such person shall produce evidence to satisfy the Council that he has been approved by the relevant authority

provisions.title: Renewal of Certificate
provisions.content: (1) Without prejudice to the provisions of the Act . any person who: (a) has been de-registered or erased from the Register, Roll or Record pursuant to the Act ; (b) has ceased to practice for three-consecutive years without paying retention fees: (c) has changed his residence or has left Tanzania; or (d) has his registration been cancelled for whatever reasons, may re-apply to the Council for renewal of registration. (2) Where the Council is satisfied with the ground for the renewal, shall, upon payment of the prescribed fee. approve re-registration of that person. (3) Where renewal of registration is granted under sub-regulation (2) , the Council may restore the certificate of Registration. Enrolment or Enlisting of such person and may impose such conditions as it may deem necessary.

provisions.title: Enrolment
provisions.content: A person shall be entitled to be enrolled under the Act if he: (a) has attained a certificate in Medical Radiology and Imaging sciences from a training institution, recognized by the Council : and (b) has completed an apprenticeship of at least nine months under the supervision of a superior Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional authorized by the Council .

provisions.title: Enlisting
provisions.content: (1) A person shall be entitled for enlisting under this Act if he satisfies the Council that: (a) he is a Sonographer who is holder of a certificate in Sonography or any other qualification recognized by the Council as being equivalent to a certificate in Sonography offered in Mainland Tanzania; (b) he has complied with such additional requirements relating to acquisition of practical experiences as a Sonographer; and (c) his professional and general conduct renders him fit and proper person to be enlisted. Part III – Provisions relating to training

provisions.title: Education and Training Committee
provisions.content: (1) Pursuant to section 7(3) of the Act , there shall be established Education and Training Committee of the Council. (2) The Education and Training Committee of the Council shall be required to inspect each Training Institution for not less than once each year to ascertain the quality of training.

provisions.title: Establishment of Radiology and Imaging Training Institution
provisions.content: No Radiology and Imaging Professional Training Institution shall be established unless it is approved by the Council to conduct radiology or imaging courses as may be determined by the Council .

provisions.title: Requirements for approval
provisions.content: (1) Any training Institution may. upon written application, be approved by the Council to conduct radiology and imaging courses as may be determined by the Council . (2) An application for Approval of radiology and imaging training institutions shall include: (a) the name and physical address of the institution; (b) the courses content and subjects content; (c) the names and numbers of lecturers or instructors with brief description of their qualifications and experience: (d) the examination and assessment procedures and processes; and (e) the facilities available to train and assess candidates.

provisions.title: Requirements for the Radiology and Imaging training Institution
provisions.content: A Radiology or Imaging training Institution shall contain; (a) adequate classrooms; (b) assembly hall capable of accommodating at least half of the whole school; (c) a demonstration room; (d) adequate practical or clinical training rooms; (e) adequate equipment for practical or clinical training; (f) library; (g) a pantry; (h) stores; (i) utilities; and (j) adequate and conducive offices for teaching staff.

provisions.title: Teaching and learning equipment
provisions.content: Each Radiology or Imaging training institution shall be required to have adequate teaching equipment and in particular shall have enough writing boards, desks, anatomical charts, skeletons, bones, models, reference books, radiology equipment, imaging equipment and such other equipment for demonstrating practical and clinical procedures.

provisions.title: Hospital facilities
provisions.content: (1) It shall be a requirement to every training institution to identify approved hospital or hospitals for practical and clinical experience of students in accordance with the curricula. (2) For the purpose of radiology or imaging training, the indentified hospital shall first be approved by the Council .

provisions.title: Qualifications offered by training institutions
provisions.content: Institutions referred to under this regulation may. depending on status and level attained, award the following: (a) certificate in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; (b) diploma in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; (c) specialized or higher Diploma in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; (d) first degree in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; (e) Master degree in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; (f) PhD in Medical Radiology and Imaging Sciences; and (g) any other award as the Council may determine.

provisions.title: Procedures related to CPD
provisions.content: (1) Radiology and Imaging training Institutions shall apply to be accredited by the Council to organise Continuing Professional Development (CPD) programmes, which will be used by the Council for Registration and retention purposes. (2) For purposes of organising CPD programme, the training Institution shall have enough facilities and faculty to organise the proposed programmes as the Council may determine. Part IV – Provisions relating to inquiries

provisions.title: Receipt of complaint by Registrar
provisions.content: Where a complaint is made by a person or body of persons charging any Medical Radiology and Imaging Professionals or a licensed person with infamous conduct in a professional respect, the complaint shall be in writing and addressed to the Registrar stating ground of the allegation and shall be accompanied with a statutory declaration verifying the facts of the case.

provisions.title: Preliminary inquiry
provisions.content: (1) In the course of preliminary inquiry, the Registrar shall have power to require the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional against whom the complaint is made or in respect of whom any information is received, for any explanation and may cause any further inquiry to be made and any further evidence to be obtained, and in that respect he may obtain or seek any advice or assistance as he thinks proper. (2) The Registrar shall provide al least seven days before an inquiry, in order to allow each member of the Council to go through the material documents pertaining to the inquiry.

provisions.title: Notice of inquiry
provisions.content: (1) Where an inquiry has been directed to be held, notice of the inquiry in the manner set out in the prescribed form shall be served to the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional concerned, and the notice shall specify the charge or charges in respect of which the inquiry shall be held, and inform the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional and the complainant of the time and place appointed for holding the inquiry.

provisions.title: Party to be supplied with in formation explanations etc.
provisions.content: Any party in an inquiry held pursuant to these Regulations shall, for the purpose of his defense or reply and upon request in writing for that purpose to the Registrar, be entitled to be supplied with a copy of any statutory declaration, explanation, answer or any other document given or submitted to the Council by or on behalf of the other party.

provisions.title: Exchange of information, materials etc.
provisions.content: The parties in an inquiry may, at any time prior to the date of holding the inquiry, serve upon each other any materials to the complaint or information or defense, as the case may be.

provisions.title: Service of Notice or documents
provisions.content: The service of' any notice or document required by these Regulations shall be deemed to be effected if— (a) served personally on the person to be served; (b) submitted by a registered post addressed to him at his latest known address in the register; or (c) in the case of service of any notice or document to a complainant or a Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or a license person, submitted by a registered post addressed to any advocate appointed by him and proof that such notice or document was served personally or was so addressed and posted, shall be proof of service.

provisions.title: Inquiry to be held in public
provisions.content: The place where the inquiry is held shall be open to the public in so far as the same can conveniently accommodate them, provided that the Council may if it deems fit and at any stage of the inquiry excludes any particular person or the public, generally.

provisions.title: Charges
provisions.content: At the opening of the inquiry, the charge or charges shall be read and if the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or an enrolled or a listed person concerned is not present, the Council shall satisfy itself that notice of the inquiry was duly served on him as prescribed under regulation 18 .

provisions.title: Proceedings at an inquiry
provisions.content: (1) The complainant shall open the case and produce his evidence in support of it. (2) The Council shall then call upon the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or enlisted person, as the case may be. to state his case and produce evidence in support of it. (3) At the conclusion of the case by the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or an enlisted person, the complainant may address the Council in reply. (4) Where the Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or an enrolled or a listed person has not produced evidence in hold up of his case, the complainant shall not, without permission of the Council , make an address in reply.

provisions.title: Evidence
provisions.content: (1) Evidence may be taken by the Council orally or in written statement, and if orally, shall be given upon oath or affirmation administered by the Chairman or if written, should be in the form of an affidavit or statutory declaration. (2) Where a witness is called by a party, he shall be first examined by the party which called him, and then be cross-examined by the other party and then be re-examined by the party which called him. (3) Where a deponent to a document is present at the inquiry and refuses to submit himself to cross-examination, the Council may refuse to admit his evidence.

provisions.title: Questions by Members of the Council
provisions.content: Members of the Council may through the Chairman, put questions to the parties or witnesses as they may think desirable.

provisions.title: Adjustment
provisions.content: (1) The Council may at any stage of the inquiry, adjourn to consider any matter arising from the deliberation of the inquiry. (2) At the Conclusion of the hearing, the Council shall deliberate on the matter in camera . (3) No person other than a member of the Council shall be entitled to be present at any meeting of the Council during an adjournment or during its deliberation, except that the legal adviser shall always be present.

provisions.title: Summary proceedings to be taken at the inquiry
provisions.content: (1) Summary of the proceedings of the inquiry, other than those related to the deliberation of which are held in camera , may be taken by a person appointed for that purpose by the Chairman, and any party who appears al the proceedings shall be entitled to inspect the transcript. (2) The Registrar shall, subject to sub regulation (1) and upon request, supply to any person entitled to appeal against the decision of the Council or to his advocate. (3) Where no summary of the proceeding is taken the Chairman or a member of the Council authorized by him in that behalf or the Registrar shall take a note of the proceedings other than those related to the deliberations held in camera and the provision of this regulation as to the inspection and taking of copies shall apply to such note.

provisions.title: Decision of the Council
provisions.content: The decision of the Council shall be signed by the Chairman under his own hand and be announced by him in public in any terms as the Council may approve.

provisions.title: Appeal on suspension or removal of the name
provisions.content: Any person who considers himself aggrieved by the decision of the Council may, within ninety days from the notification to him of such decision, appeal to the Minister.

provisions.title: Committee
provisions.content: (1) The Minister may appoint a Committee to determine appeals made under regulation 29 and give terms of reference and directions as to procedure or otherwise of the Committee as he shall think proper. (2) Any appeal to the Committee shall be presented by way of a petition stating the facts and grounds of appeal and a copy thereof shall be served to the Registrar. (3) The Committee shall, on hearing any appeal under these Regulations, have the like powers in relation to evidence, witnesses and documents as are conferred upon the Council. (4) The Committee may dismiss or allow any appeal, alter or vary the decision of the Council or make any other order as it may deem fit. (5) The Committee shall give to any Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional, an enrolled or enlisted person who appeals, an opportunity of appearing before the Committee and being heard, and may in its discretion, give audience to any other person on behalf of Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional or an enrolled or a listed person. Part V – General provisions

provisions.title: Appointment and duties of Supervision
provisions.content: (1) The Council may, pursuant to Section 48(j) of the Act and in respect of any regional or referral hospital, appoint a Senior Radiology and Imaging Professional to be a supervisor of Radiology and Imaging Professionals, Registered, Enrolled and Enlisted persons. (2) It shall be the duty of the supervisor in respect of any Radiology and Imaging Professional to: (a) Perform functions of the Council delegated to him by the Council in their areas of jurisdiction; (b) report to the council on various matters related to their activities and (c) discharge any other supervisory duties as may be directed by the Council .

provisions.title: Supervision
provisions.content: (1) Every senior Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional shall supervise an intern Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional. (2) Every Medical Radiology and Imaging Professional shall supervise enrolled and enlisted personnel in their area of jurisdiction.
